content: No curative or disease-modifying treatment exists for prion diseases. Management is entirely supportive/palliative: benzodiazepines (clonazepam) for myoclonus and agitation; antipsychotics for behavioral symptoms; analgesics for discomfort; nutritional support via feeding tube as dysphagia develops; seizure management if present. Multidisciplinary palliative care involvement early given uniformly fatal prognosis. Infection control: standard precautions sufficient for routine care; special handling for high-risk tissues (brain, spinal cord, posterior eye); use disposable instruments when possible or extended sterilization protocols (autoclave 134°C for 18 minutes or sodium hydroxide pretreatment). Experimental therapies (quinacrine, doxycycline, monoclonal antibodies) have not demonstrated clinical benefit in trials.
